title: Duplicate implementations
summary: Instead of restoring a single contract meaning, teams duplicate the same capability across environments and let them evolve separately.

services.summary: Cloud copy optimized for analytics-side enrichment.
Edge copy optimized for low-latency request handling with different defaults.

runtime_decisions: Observed two implementations of the same conceptual capability with no shared governance path.
Could not prove that profile.normalized means the same thing across edge and cloud.
Left consumers coupled to environment-specific behavior.
expectations: Duplication often feels safer than changing a drifting capability, but it creates parallel behavior that diverges further.
Cross-environment copies are especially risky because they evolve under different constraints and assumptions.
Once the same capability has multiple meanings, consumers stop sharing one coherent model.
